steps.title: Write the equation using point-slope form
steps.content:
We know the slope is $m = -\frac{5}{3}$ and the line passes through the point $(-6, 0)$. Using the point-slope form of a line:
$$y - y_1 = m(x - x_1)$$
Substitute the given point and slope:
$$y - 0 = -\frac{5}{3}(x - (-6))$$
$$y = -\frac{5}{3}(x + 6)$$

steps.title: Find the y-intercept by setting x to 0
steps.content:
The y-intercept is where the line crosses the y-axis, which occurs at $x = 0$. Substitute into our equation:
$$y = -\frac{5}{3}(0 + 6)$$
$$y = -\frac{5}{3} \cdot 6$$
$$y = -\frac{30}{3}$$
$$y = -10$$
The y-coordinate of the y-intercept is -10.
